description: Defines a plane in 3D space from a Cartesian equation ax + by + cz = d. For other construction methods, use normalplane3d, pointsplane3d, spanplane3d, interceptplane3d, or paraplane3d.
utility: Create a plane from a Cartesian equation in 3D space

arguments.name: equation
arguments.description: Cartesian equation as a quoted string - e.g., "x + y + z = 2"

alternates.description: Plane using explicit cartesian type. defer() hides the visual plane while keeping it available for operations like project3d.

alternates.syntax: plane3d(G, "x + y + z = 2", size(5), fc(lightblue), fo(0.2))
alternates.description: Plane with custom size and fill styling
Create a plane parallel to an existing plane, passing through a new point

examples.expression:
G = g3d(at(4,23), view(30, 30))
pl = plane3d(G, "x = 1", size(5.1), fc(yellow), c(gold))

G = g3d(at(4,23), view(30, 30))
p1 = plane3d(G, "x + y + z = 3", fo(0.2), fc(lightblue))
p2 = plane3d(G, "2x - y + z = 1", fo(0.2), fc(lightyellow))
